generated_oml:
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor capturing raw point cloud data at 10 Hz, transmitted over WiFi using UDP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData RawLiDARPointCloudTransmitted
]

instance SteeringAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering angle sensor measuring wheel position, transmitted over WiFi using UDP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringActuationStateTransmitted
]

instance MotorTorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor torque sensor measuring actuation state, transmitted over WiFi using UDP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationStateTransmitted
]

instance VehicleSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle speed sensor capturing dynamic state (speed, acceleration), transmitted over WiFi using UDP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleDynamicDataTransmitted
]

instance RawLiDARPointCloudTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data, serialized by ISO 23247 Data Translation FE, sent via UDP at 10 Hz."
]

instance SteeringActuationStateTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensor
    base:desc "Steering wheel actuation state, serialized by ISO 23247 Data Translation FE, sent via UDP."
]

instance MotorActuationStateTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorTorqueSensor
    base:desc "Motor actuation torque state, serialized by ISO 23247 Data Translation FE, sent via UDP."
]

instance VehicleDynamicDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleSpeedSensor
    base:desc "Vehicle dynamic state (speed, acceleration) data, serialized by ISO 23247 Data Translation FE, sent via UDP."
]

instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "LiDAR point cloud after translation, used for object tracking and ACC logic."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARPointCloudTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler, AccOffloadingEnabler
]

instance SteeringActuationStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering state after translation, used for monitoring system status."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringActuationStateTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance MotorActuationStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor torque state after translation, used for monitoring system status."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorActuationStateTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance VehicleDynamicStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle dynamic state after translation, used for monitoring and ACC logic."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleDynamicDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler, AccOffloadingEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackletsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Clustered LiDAR point cloud producing object tracklets for ACC perception."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARPointCloudTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Projected driving path derived from LiDAR clustering, used for trajectory visualization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARPointCloudTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance DataMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that streams processed data to the Data Monitoring Service."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance AccOffloadingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that forwards vehicle and sensor data to the ACC Computation Offloading Service."
    DTDFVocab:enables AccComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RemoteManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that exposes remote management controls to the Remote Management Service."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real‑time GUI visualization of vehicle state, trajectories, and object tracks, with logging and diagnostic report generation."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, ObjectTrackingInsight, AccTelemetryInsight, SaveReportAction, GenerateDiagnosticReportAction
]

instance AccComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing ACC logic (lead vehicle detection, desired speed calculation) in the cloud and streaming back control commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputeAccLogicAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service allowing operators to remotely enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, and trigger emergency braking."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, DisableACCAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into vehicle speed, acceleration, and overall dynamic state for monitoring purposes."
]

instance ObjectTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into detected objects and their tracklets derived from clustered LiDAR data."
]

instance AccTelemetryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time telemetry for Adaptive Cruise Control, including vehicle speed, gap distance, and actuator status."
]

instance SaveReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to save current vehicle state data as a report."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance GenerateDiagnosticReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to generate a diagnostic report from logged vehicle data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ComputeAccLogicAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to execute ACC logic in the cloud."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to enable the Adaptive Cruise Control system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to disable the Adaptive Cruise Control system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to set a desired cruise speed for the ACC system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to trigger emergency braking through a low‑latency network link."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands originate from the User Entity’s Control Panel and are routed to the Digital Twin’s Control Front‑End, then transmitted over the same Wi‑Fi UDP link to the Physical Twin Entity. Command payloads are formatted according to the ISO 23247 data translation schema and include ACC enable/disable directives, steering servo position set‑points, and motor torque commands. After receipt, the Physical Twin’s Actuation Front‑End validates the command, dispatches it to the vehicle’s actuator hardware, and relays an execution confirmation back to the Digital Twin via the Wi‑Fi network. The system does not define an explicit authentication or conflict resolution mechanism within the provided documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twin enforces a 100 ms clock‑synchronisation tolerance between all physical and digital components, as required by safety‑critical vehicular standards; computation off‑loading and other time‑critical services must operate within this 100 ms bound. No additional service‑level time scales are specified in the document."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows the ISO 23247 reference architecture and implements each Functional Entity as a separate ROS2 package comprising one or more ROS2 nodes. The Digital Twin entity hosts data management and control mechanisms; the Physical Twin entity contains data pre‑processing, actuation, and device‑communication functions (e.g., point‑cloud clustering and tracking); the User entity hosts visualization and control panels. Processes and data ownership are therefore distributed across distinct physical nodes, and coordination is achieved through ROS2 middleware, enabling capability sharing and state synchronization across the twin constellation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s internal communication relies on a ROS‑2 middleware (based on DDS) running in a local network; inter‑entity exchanges occur over a Wi‑Fi radio using the UDP transport layer. The system is designed to sustain a round‑trip latency of at most 100 ms for control and telemetry. No bandwidth requirements or redundancy mechanisms are specified; the network topology is a flat bus between the vehicle, digital twin server, and user interface devices."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Each component of the Digital Twin system is deployed on separate physical hardware. The Physical Twin Entity runs on the vehicle’s on‑board computer, the Digital Twin Entity executes on a server located outside the vehicle (edge or on‑premise), and the User Entity is typically accessed via a remote workstation or mobile device. Persisted telemetry, such as vehicle state history, is stored on an external storage device connected to the server. No cloud-based hosting, scaling strategies, or high‑availability measures are described in the documents."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing (Part 2 – Reference Architecture). It adopts the entity‑based reference architecture defined in the standard, consisting of an Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), a Data Collection and Device Control Entity, a Digital Twin Entity, and a User Entity. Functional Entities (FEs), which implement specific sub‑functions such as actuating the OME or transforming data, may use OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) for device‑to‑device and device‑to‑digital‑twin communication. The compliance framework ensures interoperability with manufacturing equipment by adopting standard OPC UA information models and secure transport mechanisms (e.g., TLS). The system’s adherence to ISO 23247 provides a structured approach for governance, versioning, and integration within a broader manufacturing ecosystem."
]
